title: dacryodes angulata
intro: Dacryodes angulata, also known as the East Indian walnut, is a tropical tree species native to the coastal regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Dacryodes angulata include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, as well as its ability to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Dacryodes angulata can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions.
